test_cases.description: parseTimestamp returns a numeric epoch ms value as-is
parseTimestamp parses an ISO 8601 date string into epoch ms
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns the epoch ms equivalent of the ISO string

test_cases.description: parseTimestamp returns 0 when given numeric 0 (epoch start)
parseTimestamp returns negative number as-is (pre-epoch)
parseTimestamp throws on null input
parseTimestamp throws on undefined input
parseTimestamp throws on object input
parseTimestamp throws on boolean input
parseTimestamp throws on unparseable date string
parseTimestamp return value is always finite and not NaN
test_cases.expected_output_description: Result is finite and not NaN

test_cases.description: getDefaultTTL returns 300 for direct messages
getDefaultTTL returns 3600 for private messages
getDefaultTTL returns 86400 for public messages
getDefaultTTL throws for unknown message type
getDefaultTTL throws for empty string
getDefaultTTL always returns a positive integer
test_cases.expected_output_description: All three return values are positive integers

test_cases.description: isExpired returns false for a message with full TTL remaining
isExpired returns true for a message that has exceeded its TTL
isExpired returns true when age exactly equals TTL
isExpired returns false when age is just under TTL
isExpired returns false for future timestamps (age clamped to 0)
isExpired returns true when ttl is 0 (immediately expired)
isExpired returns true (fail-safe) when timestamp is missing
isExpired returns true (fail-safe) when ttl is missing
isExpired returns true (fail-safe) when timestamp is invalid
isExpired returns true (fail-safe) for null/undefined message
isExpired always returns boolean, never throws
test_cases.expected_output_description: Always returns boolean for any input

test_cases.description: getRemainingTTL returns full TTL for freshly created message
getRemainingTTL returns half TTL when half the time has passed
getRemainingTTL returns 0 for expired message
getRemainingTTL returns full TTL for future timestamps (clamped age=0)
getRemainingTTL never returns negative value
getRemainingTTL throws on invalid timestamp
getRemainingTTL throws when ttl is missing
getRemainingTTL return value is always between 0 and message.ttl
getRebroadcastProbability returns 0.6 for freshly created message
getRebroadcastProbability returns ~0.3 when half TTL remaining
getRebroadcastProbability returns 0.0 for expired message
getRebroadcastProbability returns 0.0 when ttl is 0
getRebroadcastProbability returns 0.0 when ttl is negative
getRebroadcastProbability returns 0.6 for future timestamp (full TTL)
getRebroadcastProbability returns 0.0 on invalid input (fail-safe)
getRebroadcastProbability returns 0.0 when ttl is missing
getRebroadcastProbability never throws, always returns a number
getRebroadcastProbability is always between 0.0 and 0.6
stampTTL sets ttl=300 for direct message type
stampTTL sets ttl=3600 for private message type
stampTTL sets ttl=86400 for public message type
stampTTL returns the same object reference (mutation in place)
stampTTL preserves all existing fields on the package
test_cases.expected_output_description: foo and baz fields are unchanged

test_cases.description: stampTTL overwrites an existing ttl field
stampTTL throws for unknown message type
stampTTL throws when messagePackage is null
DEFAULT_TTLS constant has correct values: direct=300, private=3600, public=86400
test_cases.expected_output_description: All three default TTL values match the contract

test_cases.description: isExpired agrees with getRemainingTTL: expired iff remaining <= 0
